mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE declines > 0.5pp without corresponding SPX gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails: SPX returns are not significantly higher when both HOUST and RSAFS rise > 1.5% month-over-month compared to baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through inflationary pressure-driven risk-on sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines below its 12m MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, which reduces input cost pressures and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.0% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through higher discount rate expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5%, or Rank IC > 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust labor market dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer spending prospects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails: SPX returns are not significantly higher when PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp compared to baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real estate financing (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates shifting demand patterns toward construction, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sector rotation effects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails: SPX returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS declines > 2.0% and HOUST rises > 1.5% month-over-month compared to baseline (p > 0.05).
